design_philosophy.core_loop: Launch Kirby toward enemies/goal, collect Copy abilities to modify approach, defeat all enemies before final hole, compete for best stroke count and medal ranking
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Precision vs. risk—players balance accuracy with strategic enemy-hitting to gain power-ups that fundamentally change level navigation
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Miniature golf absurdism: playing as Kirby the sphere through surreal courses, gaining temporary superpowers (tornado, spark ball, UFO) that transform puzzle-solving approach
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Recontextualizes golf as a Kirby universe puzzle-combat hybrid; deliberately emphasizes Copy ability collection as core progression mechanic rather than pure golf scoring; absurdist tone with enemy-as-obstacles rather than traditional fairway design

difficulty.continues_system: Stroke-based: limited attempts per hole; failure does not prevent progression but affects medal ranking (gold/silver/bronze)
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-hole checkpointing within 8-hole courses; courses are independent stages
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High: players can choose which enemies to hit to acquire specific Copy abilities, allowing skill expression in route optimization. Medal system (gold/silver/bronze) provides graduated challenge without hard failure states

progression.upgrade_system: Copy abilities (temporary, per-level): tornado, spark ball (obstacle destruction), UFO (flight/repositioning). Abilities are collision-based power-ups found on course
progression.unlock_mechanism: Medal awards (gold/silver/bronze based on performance) unlock alternative course versions and extra features
replayability_hooks: Multiple medal tiers incentivize replaying courses for better scores
Alternative course versions unlocked by medal collection
Multiplayer vs. mode with different objectives (star collection/theft)
Copy ability routing encourages speedrunning optimization

world_building_techniques: Minimal plot: King Dedede steals stars; Kirby retrieves them via golf courses
Character recontextualization: enemies from Kirby universe function as obstacles/power-up sources
Environmental design: 8 themed courses imply progression through Kirby's world (thematic staging)

faction_indicators: Keeby (yellow palette swap) represents cooperative/competitive duality in multiplayer
danger_signals: Star theft mechanic in multiplayer (players can steal colored stars from each other)
Copy ability attacks can force opponent to skip turn, creating tension
environmental_storytelling: 8 distinct courses with thematic design imply progression through Kirby universe locations
Enemies positioned as obstacles suggest hostile environment requiring navigation skill
